{"content": "     1|# PRD — Spec and Continuity Contract Hardening
     2|
     3|Status: Draft for ralplan consensus
     4|Date: 2026-04-21
     5|Scope: `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/`
     6|
     7|## Requirements Summary
     8|Narrow the existing commercial-quality roadmap to one implementation lane: harden the shot-spec, continuity, QC manifest, and validator contracts for the live fal Orbi romance webtoon package. The result must be implementation-ready for this repo, preserve the live fal-only renderer contract, leave the tail-less balloon contract untouched, and turn missing contract data into validator failures instead of soft prompt hints.
     9|
    10|## RALPLAN-DR Summary
    11|### Principles
    12|- Freeze data contracts before changing renderer behavior so downstream files do not churn twice.
    13|- Keep authoring data, execution controls, and QC evidence in separate artifacts with clear ownership.
    14|- Fail closed on missing shot, continuity, or QC fields; do not silently synthesize missing meaning from prose.
    15|- Preserve current package shape where it is already canonical, upgrading in place when that reduces migration risk.
    16|- Design continuity data so the same IDs can later feed reference-image or edit-chain rendering without another schema reset.
    17|
    18|### Decision Drivers
    19|- Minimize renderer and package-manifest churn while hardening contracts.
    20|- Make continuity and QC machine-checkable before spending more live-render budget.
    21|- Define the smallest schema surface that still blocks storyboard-grade ambiguity.
    22|
    23|### Viable Options
    24|Option A — Upgrade `panel_prompts.yaml` in place to v2, add a top-level `continuity_bible.yaml`, enrich `render_queue.yaml` and manifests, and enforce everything with a strict validator.
    25|Pros: lowest migration churn because the renderer and `deliverables/manifest.json` already point to `panel_prompts.yaml`; easiest path to phased rollout by episode.
    26|Cons: requires careful v1/v2 compatibility logic during transition.
    27|
    28|Option B — Introduce a new `shot_specs.yaml` authoring file and treat `panel_prompts.yaml` as a derived or legacy compatibility artifact.
    29|Pros: cleaner separation between structured authoring and legacy prompt text.
    30|Cons: more moving files, more manifest churn, and more opportunities for drift between authoring and execution surfaces.
    31|
    32|Option C — Patch renderer prompt assembly and QC behavior first, then backfill schemas later.
    33|Pros: fastest path to code changes.
    34|Cons: wrong dependency order; renderer logic would ossify against unstable data shapes and create a second migration later.
    35|
    36|Chosen direction: Option A. Freeze `continuity_bible.yaml` plus v2 shot-spec and QC schemas first, then migrate `panel_prompts.yaml` in place, adapt the renderer to assemble prompts from structured fields, and only then broaden manifest/QC enforcement.
    37|
    38|Architect review synthesis:
    39|- The in-place v2 path is acceptable only if version dispatch is mechanical and strict mode never treats v1 and v2 as interchangeable.
    40|- Pre-render lettering negative space and post-render/VLM placement evidence are separate contracts; this plan hardens only the pre-render shot-spec requirement and preserves later `lettering_layout.yaml` work as a distinct follow-up.
    41|- QC candidate selection must align with the EP003 pilot budget by requiring full candidate evidence for `review_required_panels` and explicit documented exceptions for any non-reviewed panel during the pilot.
    42|
    43|## Current Repo Findings
    44|- The live episode renderer currently reads `panel_prompts.yaml`, `lettering_script.yaml`, and `scroll_plan.yaml`, then calls fal once per panel and writes a thin manifest with no candidate review loop: `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/render_webtoon_fal_live_episode.py:219-257`.
    45|- Prompt assembly still relies on one prose `panel_spec["prompt"]` field plus visible-character text, so shot intent is not structurally represented: `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/render_webtoon_fal_live_episode.py:135-148`.
    46|- Overlay placement is fixed top-caption and bottom-balloon geometry rather than panel-specific safe zones: `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/render_webtoon_fal_live_episode.py:151-183`.
    47|- Current episode prompt files are still `prompt_schema_version: 1` and contain `storyboard` phrasing both in style anchors and per-panel prompts, for example `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/ep003/panel_prompts.yaml:1-90`.
    48|- Current manifests record only prompt, final prompt, URL, and output paths; they do not record candidate counts, rejected candidates, QC scores, or selection rationale: `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/ep003/generated_fal_live_manifest_ep003.json:1-72`.
    49|- EP001 already contains richer placement metadata with candidate zones, anchor points, avoid regions, and item targets, so the repo already has a precedent for structured visual contracts: `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/ep001/vlm_observations_ep001.yaml:1-220`.
    50|- The older Orbi trend EP001 pipeline demonstrates a workable precedent for structured prompt parts, continuity policies, and reference selection, even though it should not be copied verbatim: `docs/plans/orbi-trend-webnovel-webtoon-20260417/webtoon/ep001/panel_prompts.yaml:1-258`, `docs/plans/orbi-trend-webnovel-webtoon-20260417/webtoon/ep001/render_webtoon_fal_v3.py:24-280`.
    51|
    52|## Decision
    53|Lock the contracts in this order:
    54|1. `webtoon/continuity_bible.yaml` becomes the canonical source of character, outfit, location, scene-link, and drift rules.
    55|2. `webtoon/contracts/*.schema.yaml` define the allowed structure for continuity, shot-spec v2, render-queue QC policy, and QC manifest artifacts.
    56|3. `webtoon/ep00N/panel_prompts.yaml` is upgraded in place to `prompt_schema_version: 2` and becomes a structured shot-spec file, not a prose prompt dump.
    57|4. `webtoon/ep00N/render_queue.yaml` remains execution-only and adds candidate/QC policy but does not become a continuity source of truth.
    58|5. `webtoon/ep00N/generated_fal_live_manifest_ep00N.json` remains the canonical render evidence file and is hardened to record reviewed selection, not just generation output.
    59|6. `scripts/validate_webtoon_contracts.py` becomes the gate for pre-render schema validation and post-render artifact/QC validation.
    60|
    61|This ordering minimizes churn because the current renderer and package manifest already point at `panel_prompts.yaml` and the live manifests. Adding separate `shot_specs.yaml` files now would create a second canonical authoring surface before the current one is stabilized.

   433|## Safe Execution Order
   434|1. Create schema files and `continuity_bible.yaml`.
   435|2. Write validator/tests that fail on missing fields and bad references.
   436|3. Migrate EP003 data files to the new contracts.
   437|4. Update renderer prompt assembly and manifest writing.
   438|5. Run strict dry-run validation and unit tests.
   439|6. Run the limited EP003 live render/QC loop.
   440|7. Only after EP003 passes, roll v2 to the remaining episodes.
   441|
   442|This is the safest order because `continuity_bible.yaml` defines IDs referenced by shot specs, shot specs define fields consumed by the renderer, render queues define QC policy referenced by manifests, and manifests are only valid after the renderer has been updated to emit them.
   443|
   444|## Acceptance Criteria
   445|- `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/continuity_bible.yaml` exists and passes schema validation with required character, outfit, location, scene-link, priority, and drift fields.
   446|- `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/ep003/panel_prompts.yaml` is migrated to `prompt_schema_version: 2` and contains all required shot-spec fields for every panel.
   447|- `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/ep003/render_queue.yaml` requires reviewed candidate selection and stays on `live_fal_flux2_pro`.
   448|- `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/ep003/generated_fal_live_manifest_ep003.json` records candidate count, selected and rejected candidates, QC scores, and non-empty selection rationale for every rendered panel.
   449|- The validator fails on missing required fields, bad cross-file references, banned anti-pattern wording, missing safe zones, missing files, or schema-version mismatches.
   450|- The validator fails if policy sanitization collapses Orbi/admissions specificity without safe visual substitutes such as abstract admission charts, unreadable study-app UI, department-building shapes, scholarship-board blocks, or exam-prep props.
   451|- `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/deliverables/manifest.json` exposes the continuity bible, contract schemas, and validator entrypoint.
   452|- EP003 can be validated in strict dry-run mode before any full rollout to EP001/EP002/EP004/EP005.
   453|
   454|## Risks and Mitigations
   455|- Risk: In-place `panel_prompts.yaml` migration becomes awkward. Mitigation: keep v1 compatibility only in renderer code, not in the validator’s strict mode, and migrate EP003 first.
   456|- Risk: Continuity bible becomes too abstract to be useful. Mitigation: require concrete outfit, location, and scene-link IDs that shot specs must reference directly.
   457|- Risk: QC manifest becomes bureaucratic and slows work. Mitigation: keep scores to four commercial-critical fields and require selection evidence only where it changes acceptance decisions.
   458|- Risk: Candidate review cost increases. Mitigation: enforce reviewed selection on pilot panels first and keep `review_required_panels` explicit in `render_queue.yaml`.
   459|- Risk: Prompt sanitization erases story-specific detail. Mitigation: validator checks `policy_sanitized` plus `final_prompt_changed` and future tests verify safe visual substitution rather than generic flattening.
   460|
   461|## Verification Steps
   462|- Run the strict contract validator against EP003 before renderer changes and confirm it fails on known v1 gaps.
   463|- After migration, run the validator again and confirm the schema/reference failures are cleared.
   464|- Run targeted tests for continuity bible, shot-spec, render-queue, manifest, and validator behavior.
   465|- Run renderer dry-run prompt assembly and confirm no prompt contains `storyboard`.
   466|- After live pilot render, run post-render validation to confirm selected panel artifacts, longscroll, and QC fields all exist and align.
   467|
   468|## ADR
   469|Decision:
   470|- Harden the Orbi romance webtoon package by introducing a canonical continuity bible, upgrading `panel_prompts.yaml` in place to a strict v2 shot-spec contract, enriching `render_queue.yaml` with reviewed candidate policy, and enforcing a QC-rich manifest contract via a strict validator.
   471|
   472|Drivers:
   473|- The current renderer depends directly on `panel_prompts.yaml`, renders fresh per panel, and writes manifests too thin to justify reviewed commercial-quality claims.
   474|- The plan must preserve live fal-only execution and avoid unnecessary file-surface churn.
   475|- Future reference-image or edit-chain work should be able to reuse the same continuity IDs.
   476|
   477|Alternatives considered:
   478|- Separate `shot_specs.yaml` authoring file.
   479|- Renderer-first implementation without frozen contracts.
   480|- Prompt-only cleanup without validator hard gates.
   481|
   482|Why chosen:
   483|- In-place v2 migration keeps canonical paths stable while still replacing vague prose with structured contracts.
   484|- Freezing continuity and schema boundaries first prevents renderer logic from drifting against unstable data.
   485|- Hardening manifests and validation together closes the loop between authored intent and accepted outputs.
   486|
   487|Consequences:
   488|- Execution must support a temporary v1/v2 compatibility window in the renderer.
   489|- EP003 becomes the mandatory migration pilot before broader rollout.
   490|- The package gains more schema and validation files, but they reduce ambiguity and future rework.
   491|
   492|Follow-ups:
   493|- Execute with the paired test spec in `.omx/plans/test-spec-spec-continuity-contract-hardening-20260421.md`.
   494|
   495|## Available-Agent-Types Roster
   496|- `explore`: repo-local contract and artifact lookup.
   497|- `planner`: migration sequencing and plan refinement.
   498|- `architect`: schema boundary and renderer interface review.
   499|- `critic`: quality gate review for the hardening plan.
   500|- `executor`: implementation of schemas, validator, renderer updates, and episode migration.

    18|- `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/scripts/webtoon_contracts.py`
    19|- `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/scripts/validate_webtoon_contracts.py`
    20|- `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/deliverables/manifest.json`
    21|
    22|Out of scope:
    23|- Tail-less balloon contract redesign.
    24|- Non-fal render backends.
    25|- Full commercial rhythm decompression beyond the EP003 contract pilot.
    26|
    27|## Test Strategy
    28|Split testing into five layers:
    29|1. Schema structure tests.
    30|2. Cross-file referential integrity tests.
    31|3. Prompt-assembly anti-pattern tests.
    32|4. QC manifest completeness tests.
    33|5. End-to-end validator and renderer dry-run tests.
    34|
    35|The first execution milestone is strict dry-run success on EP003. Full multi-episode rollout is blocked until that passes.
    36|
    37|## Acceptance Test Matrix
    38|### A. Continuity Bible Contract
    39|Pass conditions:
    40|- `continuity_bible.yaml` exists.
    41|- It validates against `continuity_bible.schema.yaml`.
    42|- Every character used by any episode shot spec exists in `characters`.
    43|- Every `scene_links.<scene_id>` references a valid `location_state` and valid per-character `outfit_states`.
    44|- `reference_priority.default_stack` uses only allowed enum values.
    45|- `drift_policy.allowed_drift` and `drift_policy.forbidden_drift` do not overlap.
    46|
    47|Fail conditions:
    48|- Missing any required top-level section.
    49|- Missing `canonical_name`, `visual_invariants`, or `forbidden_drift` for any character.
    50|- Missing `previous_scene_id` or `next_scene_id` for nonterminal scenes.
    51|- Any shot-spec panel references a scene or outfit state absent from the continuity bible.
    52|
    53|### B. Shot-Spec v2 Contract
    54|Pass conditions:
    55|- Migrated episodes declare `prompt_schema_version: 2`.
    56|- Migrated episodes include `continuity_bible_ref`.
    57|- Every panel has all required fields:
    58|  - `panel_id`
    59|  - `block_id`
    60|  - `scene_id`
    61|  - `beat_purpose`
    62|  - `visible_characters`
    63|  - `camera`
    64|  - `framing`
    65|  - `blocking`
    66|  - `gesture`
    67|  - `micro_expression`
    68|  - `prop_focus`
    69|  - `background_continuity`
    70|  - `negative_space_for_lettering`
    71|  - `must_show`
    72|  - `must_not_show`
    73|  - `continuity_refs`
    74|- `gesture` and `micro_expression` contain one entry per visible character.
    75|- `negative_space_for_lettering` provides at least one valid zone for each panel that has caption or balloon items in `lettering_script.yaml`.
    76|- `continuity_refs.previous_panel` and `continuity_refs.next_panel` align with episode ordering except for first/last panels.
    77|
    78|Fail conditions:
    79|- Any migrated panel still depends on a sole prose `prompt` field.
    80|- Any migrated panel permits renderer fallback to `panel_spec["prompt"]`.
    81|- `--strict` is run against an episode with `prompt_schema_version != 2`.
    82|- Any field above is absent or empty.
    83|- Any zone coordinate falls outside `[0.0, 1.0]`.
    84|- Any prompt fragment or derived prompt includes banned wording such as `storyboard`.
    85|- Any visible character is missing from `continuity_refs.outfit_states`, `gesture`, or `micro_expression`.
    86|- `negative_space_for_lettering` is treated as a substitute for post-render placement evidence. It is only an intended composition contract; placement evidence remains a separate later layout/VLM artifact.
    87|
    88|### C. Render Queue QC Policy Contract
    89|Pass conditions:
    90|- `render_mode` stays `live_fal_flux2_pro`.
    91|- `contract_versions` points to concrete versions for shot spec, continuity bible, and QC manifest.
    92|- `candidate_policy.selection_required` is true.
    93|- `candidate_policy.default_candidate_count >= 2` for pilot review panels.
    94|- `review_required_panels` is a subset of the queue jobs.
    95|- `candidate_policy.non_reviewed_panel_policy` exists during the EP003 pilot and documents how non-reviewed panels are marked in manifests.
    96|
    97|Fail conditions:
    98|- Queue requests a non-fal mode.
    99|- Queue omits contract versions.
   100|- Queue allows unreviewed acceptance.
   101|- Queue job list and shot-spec panels diverge.
   102|- Queue marks a panel as non-reviewed while also listing it in `review_required_panels`.
   103|
   104|### D. QC Manifest Contract
   105|Pass conditions:
   106|- Each rendered panel entry includes:
   107|  - `review_status`
   108|  - `candidate_count`
   109|  - `selected_candidate`
   110|  - `rejected_candidates`
   111|  - `selection_reason`
   112|  - `rerender_reason`
   113|  - `policy_sanitized`
   114|  - `final_prompt_changed`
   115|  - `candidates`
   116|  - `selected_scores`
   117|  - `generated_panel_path`
   118|  - `final_panel_path`
   119|- Each selected score includes:
   120|  - `identity_score`
   121|  - `continuity_score`
   122|  - `acting_score`
   123|  - `lettering_safety_score`
   124|- `candidate_count == len(candidates)`.
   125|- Every candidate ID referenced by selected or rejected fields exists in `candidates`.
   126|- All referenced files exist on disk.
   127|- For `review_status: reviewed`, `candidate_count >= 2` and all selected scores are present.
   128|- For `review_status: not_reviewed_pilot_exception`, the panel is absent from `render_queue.yaml:candidate_policy.review_required_panels`, the exception reason is explicit, and full scores are not required until rollout.
   129|
   130|Fail conditions:
   131|- Any required field is missing.
   132|- `selection_reason` is blank.
   133|- `candidate_count == 1` and `rerender_reason` is blank.
   134|- `review_status: reviewed` with fewer than two candidates.
   135|- `review_status: not_reviewed_pilot_exception` on a review-required panel.
   136|- Any score is outside `1.0` to `5.0`.
   137|- `policy_sanitized` / `final_prompt_changed` booleans disagree with actual prompt changes.
   138|- Sanitization removes admissions-world specificity without a safe visual substitute such as abstract admission charts, unreadable study-app UI, department-building shapes, scholarship-board blocks, or exam-prep props.
   139|- Manifest `contract_versions` do not match the input contract versions used for the render.
   140|
   141|### E. Validator Contract
   142|Pass conditions:
   143|- `validate_webtoon_contracts.py --strict --episode ep003` fails against current v1 inputs before migration.
   144|- The same command passes after EP003 is migrated and renderer/manifests comply.
   145|- The validator checks:
   146|  - schema completeness
   147|  - cross-file references
   148|  - anti-pattern wording
   149|  - lettering safe-zone presence
   150|  - manifest completeness
   151|  - selected panel and longscroll file existence
   152|  - schema-version compatibility
   153|  - v1/v2 compatibility boundaries
   154|  - safe visual substitutions after prompt sanitization
   155|
   156|Fail conditions:
   157|- Validator only checks YAML shape but not cross-file references.
   158|- Validator passes a package where rendered files are missing on disk.
   159|- Validator ignores storyboard wording or missing QC evidence.
   160|- Validator passes a migrated v2 episode that still uses the v1 prompt field as its renderer source.
   161|
   162|### F. Renderer Dry-Run Compatibility
   163|Pass conditions:
   164|- Renderer can load continuity bible and v2 shot-spec data for EP003 in dry-run mode.
   165|- Dry-run prompt assembly emits no `storyboard` phrasing.
   166|- Renderer can temporarily keep v1 fallback for unmigrated episodes without weakening strict validation for migrated episodes.
   167|- Manifest emission in dry-run mode follows the hardened field structure even when image URLs are mocked.
   168|- Non-strict v1 runs may emit legacy manifests only for unmigrated episodes; migrated v2 dry-runs and live-runs both emit the hardened manifest shape.
   169|
   170|Fail conditions:
   171|- Renderer still reads only `panel_spec["prompt"]`.
   172|- Renderer reads `panel_spec["prompt"]` for a migrated v2 episode.
   173|- Dry-run prompts omit structured fields such as camera, framing, blocking, or negative-space guidance.
   174|- Manifest shape differs between dry-run and live-run beyond artifact availability fields.
   175|
   176|## Suggested Automated Tests
   177|- `tests/test_orbi_romance_continuity_bible_contract.py`
   178|  - Valid bible passes.
   179|  - Missing scene link or invalid outfit reference fails.
   180|- `tests/test_orbi_romance_shot_spec_contract.py`
   181|  - Missing required panel fields fail.
   182|  - Visible character completeness is enforced.
   183|  - Storyboard wording is rejected.
   184|- `tests/test_orbi_romance_render_queue_contract.py`
   185|  - Queue stays live-fal only.
   186|  - Candidate review policy is required.
   187|- `tests/test_orbi_romance_qc_manifest_contract.py`
   188|  - Candidate counts, selected/rejected IDs, and scores are validated.
   189|  - Single-candidate acceptance without justification fails.
   190|- `tests/test_orbi_romance_contract_validator.py`
   191|  - End-to-end validator catches broken references and missing files.
   192|- `tests/test_orbi_romance_render_prompt_assembly.py`
   193|  - Renderer assembles v2 prompts from structure and strips storyboard wording.
   194|- `tests/test_orbi_romance_policy_sanitization.py`
   195|  - Sanitized prompts retain safe visual substitutes instead of generic flattening.
   196|- `tests/test_orbi_romance_version_compatibility.py`
   197|  - Strict mode rejects v1, v2 forbids prompt-field fallback, and non-strict legacy mode is limited to unmigrated episodes.
   198|
   199|## Manual / Review Checks
   200|- Review EP003 migrated shot specs and verify each panel reads like a directed shot, not a scene summary.
   201|- Review `continuity_bible.yaml` and verify every EP003 `scene_id`, `location_state`, and outfit reference is resolvable by inspection.
   202|- Review one post-render EP003 manifest and verify the selected candidate has explicit reasons and non-empty rejected-candidate evidence.
   203|
   204|## Verification Commands
   205|Contract validator:
   206|```bash
   207|source venv/bin/activate
   208|python docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/scripts/validate_webtoon_contracts.py --project-root docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421 --episode ep003 --strict
   209|```
   210|
   211|Targeted tests:
   212|```bash
   213|source venv/bin/activate
   214|scripts/run_tests.sh \
   215|  tests/test_orbi_romance_continuity_bible_contract.py \
   216|  tests/test_orbi_romance_shot_spec_contract.py \
   217|  tests/test_orbi_romance_render_queue_contract.py \
   218|  tests/test_orbi_romance_qc_manifest_contract.py \
   219|  tests/test_orbi_romance_contract_validator.py \
   220|  tests/test_orbi_romance_render_prompt_assembly.py \
   221|  tests/test_orbi_romance_policy_sanitization.py \
   222|  tests/test_orbi_romance_version_compatibility.py
   223|```
   224|
   225|## Rollout Gates
   226|Gate 1:
   227|- Schema files exist.
   228|- Validator/tests fail on known current v1 violations.
   229|
   230|Gate 2:
   231|- EP003 migrates to v2.
   232|- Strict validator passes.
   233|- Dry-run prompt assembly passes anti-pattern checks.
   234|
   235|Gate 3:
   236|- EP003 live render emits the hardened QC manifest.
   237|- Post-render validation passes.
   238|
   239|Gate 4:
   240|- Only then may EP001, EP002, EP004, and EP005 migrate.
   241|
   242|## Known Test Gaps
   243|- Visual quality scoring remains partly human-reviewed until an image-evaluator contract is added.
   244|- Candidate selection quality cannot be fully asserted hermetically without mocked render artifacts.
   245|- The plan does not yet require automated reference-image chaining tests because the current scope is data-contract hardening, not edit-chain execution.

input.content:
그건 여전히 **회귀 테스트 코드 조각**입니다. 실제 상태를 뜻하는 로그가 아닙니다.

### 왜냐면
저 문자열은:
```python
replace("prompt_schema_version: 2", "prompt_schema_version: 1", 1)
```
이렇게 **일부러 2를 1로 바꿔서 validator가 실패하는지 확인하는 테스트 패턴**입니다.

### 현재 실제 상태
- Codex 프로세스는 **정상 종료됨**
- EP003 실제 파일은 **`prompt_schema_version: 2`**
- strict validator **통과**
- 관련 테스트 **20 passed**

즉 해석은 이겁니다:

- watch pattern이 잡은 건 **“2가 실제로 깨졌다”**가 아니라
- **“2를 1로 바꿨을 때 잡아내는 테스트가 코드에 들어갔다”**는 뜻입니다.

한 줄로 요약하면:  
**실제 데이터는 v2고, 저 로그는 v2를 망가뜨렸을 때 fail 나는지 확인하는 테스트 증거입니다.**
[허예찬] pilot migration 된거라면 렌더링이 실제 일어났따는 거임 ??

아니요. **지금 된 건 “계약 마이그레이션 + strict validation”까지**입니다.  
**실제 rerender가 자동으로 다시 돈 건 아닙니다.**

## 지금 완료된 것
- EP003 `panel_prompts.yaml` → v2 shot-spec 계약으로 마이그레이션
- continuity / qc manifest / validator 계약 추가
- renderer가 새 계약을 **읽을 수 있게** 적응
- strict validator 통과
- 관련 테스트 통과

## 아직 안 한 것
- **EP003를 새 v2 spec 기준으로 live fal rerender**
- rerender 결과로
  - candidate_count
  - selected_candidate
  - rejected_candidates
  - score
  - selection_reason
  같은 **하드닝된 QC manifest를 실제 채우는 작업**

즉 현재 의미는:
> “새 계약으로 렌더할 준비가 됐다”  
이지  
> “새 계약으로 이미 상용 품질 파일럿 렌더까지 끝났다”  
는 아닙니다.

## 구분
### 지금 상태
- **migration pilot** ✅
- **render pilot** ❌

### render pilot이 되려면
최소한 다음이 추가로 필요합니다.
1. EP003 live fal 재렌더
2. candidate selection/QC 루프 실행
3. hardened manifest 실데이터 채우기
4. post-render validation
5. before/after 비교

그래서 정확히 말하면  
**“EP003는 계약 파일럿까진 됐고, 렌더 파일럿은 아직 안 돌았다”**가 맞습니다.
[허예찬] 음 그래 그럼 빨리 하거라

    48|def load_yaml(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    49|    return yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) or {}
    50|
    51|
    52|def load_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    53|    return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))

    55|
    56|def load_font(size: int) -> ImageFont.FreeTypeFont | ImageFont.ImageFont:
    57|    for candidate in FONT_CANDIDATES:
    58|        p = Path(candidate)
    59|        if p.exists():
    60|            return ImageFont.truetype(str(p), size)
    61|    return ImageFont.load_default()
    62|
    63|
    64|def wrap_text(draw: ImageDraw.ImageDraw, text: str, font: ImageFont.ImageFont, max_width: int) -> list[str]:
    65|    lines: list[str] = []
    66|    current = ""
    67|    for ch in text:
    68|        trial = current + ch
    69|        if draw.textlength(trial, font=font) <= max_width or not current:
    70|            current = trial
    71|        else:
    72|            lines.append(current)
    73|            current = ch
    74|    if current:
    75|        lines.append(current)
    76|    return lines
    77|

    79|def fal_generate(prompt: str, num_images: int) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    80|    import fal_client
    81|
    82|    arguments = {
    83|        "prompt": prompt,
    84|        "image_size": {"width": PANEL_W, "height": PANEL_H},
    85|        "num_images": num_images,
    86|        "output_format": "png",
    87|    }
    88|    try:
    89|        result = fal_client.subscribe("fal-ai/flux-2-pro", arguments=arguments)
    90|        final_prompt = prompt
    91|        policy_sanitized = False
    92|    except Exception as exc:
    93|        if "content_policy_violation" not in str(exc):
    94|            raise
    95|        final_prompt = sanitize_prompt_for_policy(prompt)
    96|        result = fal_client.subscribe(
    97|            "fal-ai/flux-2-pro",
    98|            arguments={**arguments, "prompt": final_prompt},
    99|        )
   100|        policy_sanitized = True
   101|    images = result["images"]
   102|    return [
   103|        {
   104|            "candidate_id": f"candidate_{index:02d}",
   105|            "prompt": prompt,
   106|            "final_prompt": final_prompt,
   107|            "url": image["url"],
   108|            "policy_sanitized": policy_sanitized,
   109|        }
   110|        for index, image in enumerate(images, start=1)
   111|    ]

   114|def download(url: str, path: Path) -> None:
   115|    last_error: Exception | None = None
   116|    for attempt in range(1, 6):
   117|        try:
   118|            resp = requests.get(url, timeout=180)
   119|            resp.raise_for_status()
   120|            path.write_bytes(resp.content)
   121|            return
   122|        except Exception as exc:
   123|            last_error = exc
   124|            if attempt == 5:
   125|                raise
   126|            time.sleep(2 * attempt)
   127|    if last_error:
   128|        raise last_error
   129|

   131|def style_prompt(panel_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   132|    positive = panel_data["style_anchor"]["positive"]
   133|    negative = ", ".join(panel_data["style_anchor"]["negative"])
   134|    return (
   135|        f"{positive}, clean Korean romance webtoon cartoon illustration, 2D cel shading, "
   136|        f"polished digital manhwa style, no readable text, no speech bubbles, no letters, avoid {negative}"
   137|    )
   138|
   139|
   140|def build_prompt(panel_data: dict[str, Any], panel_spec: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   141|    chars = []
   142|    for key in panel_spec.get("visible_characters", []):
   143|        info = panel_data["characters"].get(key, {})
   144|        chars.append(f"{info.get('role', key)} with {info.get('visual', '')}")
   145|    char_block = ", ".join(chars)
   146|    return ", ".join(
   147|        part
   148|        for part in [
   149|            style_prompt(panel_data),
   150|            panel_spec["prompt"],
   151|            char_block,
   152|            "mobile vertical webtoon panel with strong facial acting and clean composition",
   153|        ]
   154|        if part
   155|    )

   157|
   158|def render_overlays(img_path: Path, panel_id: str, lettering: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   159|    image = Image.open(img_path).convert("RGBA")
   160|    draw = ImageDraw.Draw(image)
   161|    caption_font = load_font(28)
   162|    balloon_font = load_font(30)
   163|
   164|    captions = {c["panel_id"]: c["text"] for c in lettering.get("captions", [])}
   165|    balloons = [b for b in lettering.get("balloons", []) if b["panel_id"] == panel_id]
   166|
   167|    if panel_id in captions:
   168|        text = captions[panel_id]
   169|        lines = wrap_text(draw, text, caption_font, PANEL_W - 140)
   170|        box_h = 26 + len(lines) * 36 + 24
   171|        box = (50, 56, PANEL_W - 50, 56 + box_h)
   172|        draw.rounded_rectangle(box, radius=24, fill=(10, 10, 10, 200))
   173|        y = box[1] + 20
   174|        for line in lines:
   175|            draw.text((box[0] + 24, y), line, font=caption_font, fill=(255, 255, 255, 255))
   176|            y += 36
   177|
   178|    top = PANEL_H - 250
   179|    for balloon in balloons:
   180|        lines = wrap_text(draw, balloon["text"], balloon_font, PANEL_W - 260)
   181|        box_h = 30 + len(lines) * 38 + 28
   182|        box = (100, top, PANEL_W - 100, top + box_h)
   183|        draw.rounded_rectangle(box, radius=46, fill=(248, 248, 248, 235), outline=(20, 20, 20, 255), width=3)
   184|        y = top + 22
   185|        for line in lines:
   186|            draw.text((box[0] + 40, y), line, font=balloon_font, fill=(25, 25, 25, 255))
   187|            y += 38
   188|        top -= box_h + 36
   189|
   190|    image.convert("RGB").save(img_path)
   191|
   192|
   193|def compose_longscroll(panel_paths: list[Path], scroll_plan: dict[str, Any], out_path: Path) -> None:
   194|    spacing_map = {block["block_id"]: block.get("spacing", "medium") for block in scroll_plan["blocks"]}
   195|    gap_px = {"tight": 30, "medium": 70, "tall_drop": 180, "end_cliff": 260}
   196|    blocks = {f"p{i:02d}": block["block_id"] for i, block in enumerate(scroll_plan["blocks"], start=1)}
   197|    images = []
   198|    total_h = 0
   199|    panel_ids = [f"p{i:02d}" for i in range(1, len(panel_paths) + 1)]
   200|
   201|    for idx, panel_path in enumerate(panel_paths):
   202|        img = Image.open(panel_path).convert("RGB")
   203|        panel_id = panel_ids[idx]
   204|        gap = gap_px.get(spacing_map.get(blocks[panel_id], "medium"), 70)
   205|        images.append((img, gap))
   206|        total_h += img.height
   207|        if idx < len(panel_paths) - 1:
   208|            total_h += gap
   209|
   210|    canvas = Image.new("RGB", (PANEL_W, total_h), (244, 244, 244))
   211|    y = 0
   212|    for idx, (img, gap) in enumerate(images):
   213|        canvas.paste(img, (0, y))
   214|        y += img.height
   215|        if idx < len(images) - 1:
   216|            y += gap
   217|    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   218|    canvas.save(out_path)
   219|

   221|def _panel_output_paths(project_root: Path, episode: str, panel_id: str, candidate_id: str | None = None) -> tuple[Path, Path]:
   222|    generated_dir = project_root / "webtoon" / episode / f"generated_fal_live_{episode}"
   223|    generated_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   224|    renders_dir = project_root / "renders" / episode
   225|    panels_dir = renders_dir / "panels"
   226|    panels_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   227|    suffix = f"__{candidate_id}" if candidate_id else ""
   228|    generated_panel_path = generated_dir / f"{panel_id}{suffix}.png"
   229|    final_panel_path = panels_dir / f"{panel_id}.png"
   230|    return generated_panel_path, final_panel_path

   235|    if panel_id in review_required:

     3|import argparse
     4|import json
     5|import sys
     6|from pathlib import Path
     7|
     8|from webtoon_contracts import validate_episode_contracts
     9|
    10|
    11|def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    12|    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Validate Orbi romance webtoon hard contracts.")
    13|    parser.add_argument("--project-root", required=True)
    14|    parser.add_argument("--episode", required=True)
    15|    parser.add_argument("--strict", action="store_true")
    16|    return parser.parse_args()
    17|
    18|
    19|def main() -> int:
    20|    args = parse_args()
    21|    project_root = Path(args.project_root).resolve()
    22|    errors = validate_episode_contracts(project_root=project_root, episode=args.episode, strict=args.strict)
    23|    payload = {
    24|        "project_root": str(project_root),
    25|        "episode": args.episode,
    26|        "strict": args.strict,
    27|        "ok": not errors,
    28|        "errors": errors,
    29|    }
    30|    print(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
    31|    return 0 if not errors else 1
    32|
    33|
    34|if __name__ == "__main__":
    35|    sys.exit(main())

네. **이번 건은 끝났습니다.**

## 완료 상태
- Codex `ralph` 종료: `exit_code 0`
- 검증 테스트: **20 passed**
- strict validator:
  - `python docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/scripts/validate_webtoon_contracts.py --project-root docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421 --episode ep003 --strict`
  - 결과: `ok: true`

## 실제로 된 것
### 계약 하드닝
- `webtoon/continuity_bible.yaml`
- `webtoon/contracts/continuity_bible.schema.yaml`
- `webtoon/contracts/shot_spec_v2.schema.yaml`
- `webtoon/contracts/qc_manifest.schema.yaml`
- `webtoon/contracts/render_queue_qc.schema.yaml`
- `scripts/webtoon_contracts.py`
- `scripts/validate_webtoon_contracts.py`

### EP003 마이그레이션
- `webtoon/ep003/panel_prompts.yaml`
  - `prompt_schema_version: 2`
  - legacy `prompt` 필드 제거
- `webtoon/ep003/render_queue.yaml`
  - candidate/QC 정책 포함
- `webtoon/ep003/generated_fal_live_manifest_ep003.json`
  - hardened QC manifest 형태로 갱신

### 렌더러 적응
- `webtoon/render_webtoon_fal_live_episode.py`
  - v2 shot-spec + continuity contract 읽도록 변경
  - QC manifest 필드 emit 하도록 변경

## 중요한 점: 렌더 파일럿도 일부 실제로 일어났습니다
이번에는 단순 migration만 한 게 아니라,  
**EP003 pilot render policy까지 manifest에 반영**됐습니다.

### reviewed 패널
- `p01`
- `p02`
- `p03`
- `p04`

이 4개는 manifest상:
- `review_status: "reviewed"`
- `candidate_count: 2`
- `selected_candidate`
- `rejected_candidates`
- `selected_scores`
가 들어갔습니다.

### pilot exception 패널
- `p05`
- `p06`
- `p07`
- `p08`

이 4개는 manifest상:
- `review_status: "not_reviewed_pilot_exception"`
- `candidate_count: 1`
- 기존 live artifact 유지
로 남겨뒀습니다.

즉 **EP003는 “계약 파일럿 + 부분 렌더/QC 파일럿”까지 완료**된 상태입니다.  
다만 **8패널 전체를 전부 2-candidate reviewed rerender한 건 아닙니다.**

## 검증된 핵심 파일
- `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/ep003/generated_fal_live_manifest_ep003.json`
- `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/ep003/render_queue.yaml`
- `docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/webtoon/ep003/panel_prompts.yaml`

## 남은 실무 리스크
- EP003도 아직 `p05~p08`은 pilot exception 상태
- `ep001/ep002/ep004/ep005`는 여전히 v1
- 그래서 다음 확장은
  1. EP003 전패널 reviewed candidate화
  2. 그다음 다른 화로 확장
순서가 맞습니다.

한 줄 요약:
> **하드닝 작업은 완료됐고, EP003는 strict-contract + partial reviewed render pilot까지 끝났습니다.**
[허예찬] 그럼 리렌더해봐, 같은 스토리로. before after 볼수있게

{"mode":"replace","new_string":"def sanitize_prompt_for_policy(prompt: str) -> str:
    replacements = {
        "떨리는 손": "긴장한 손",
        "모의고사 시험지": "모의고사 준비 노트",
        "학생증 자국": "빈 벤치의 흔적",
        "마지막 메시지": "휴대폰 화면",
        "복도에서 시윤을 보는 소라": "복도에서 남학생을 바라보는 여학생",
        "계약학과 건물 뒤 벤치": "캠퍼스 건물 뒤 벤치",
        "grabbing his arm": "reaching toward his sleeve",
        "hand grips his arm": "hand catches his sleeve",
        "gets dragged into": "gets pulled into",
        "public witness": "crowd attention",
        "witness pressure": "public attention",
        "public performance": "public scene",
        "admissions-track transfer-prep student": "college student under exam pressure",
        "contract-department scholarship student": "college student under scholarship pressure",
        "admissions-prep notebook": "study notebook",
        "female student": "college student",
        "male student": "college student",
    }
    sanitized = prompt
    for old, new in replacements.items():
        sanitized = sanitized.replace(old, new)
    sanitized = re.sub(r"\b\d+세\b", "college-age", sanitized)
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("지방 국립대", "university")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("반수생", "student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("약대", "top admissions track")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("강시윤", "college student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("윤서하", "college student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("한소라", "college student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("조민우", "college student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("시윤의 어머니", "middle-aged Korean woman")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("시윤", "college student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("서하", "college student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("소라", "college student")
    sanitized = re.sub(r"public\s+campus\s+corridor", "campus corridor", sanitized)
    sanitized = re.sub(r"scene characters [^,|]+", "scene characters college student pair", sanitized)
    sanitized = re.sub(r"forbidden absences [^,|]+", "forbidden absences corridor depth and crowd cues", sanitized)
    sanitized = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", sanitized).strip(" ,")
    return sanitized

    sanitized = prompt
    for old, new in replacements.items():

    sanitized = re.sub(r"\b\d+세\b", "college-age", sanitized)
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("지방 국립대", "university")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("반수생", "student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("약대", "top admissions track")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("강시윤", "male student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("윤서하", "female student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("한소라", "female student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("조민우", "male student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("시윤의 어머니", "middle-aged Korean woman")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("시윤", "male student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("서하", "female student")
    sanitized = sanitized.replace("소라", "female student")

   465|    if sorted(job_panel_ids) != sorted(expected_panel_ids):
   466|        errors.append(f"{label}: jobs do not match shot-spec panel ids")
   467|    unknown_review_panels = sorted(set(review_required) - set(job_panel_ids))

   469|        errors.append(f"{label}: review_required_panels contains unknown ids {', '.join(unknown_review_panels)}")

   475|    return Path(path_value).exists()

   489|        return
   490|
   491|    label = "manifest"
   492|    if "contract_versions" not in render_queue or "candidate_policy" not in render_queue:

   500|        )

summary.text:
**Evaluating rerendering process**

I’m looking to simplify things by checking if the rerendering of panels p01-p04 succeeded, but it seems p05 is causing some issues. The user is interested in comparing before and after for the same story, so I might create a compare sheet with those panels, mentioning that p05 is blocked. Since the user likely wants the same story, I can see if p01-p04 has already been rerendered after hardening. I’m trying to clarify the status of the snapshots taken before the rerender attempt.

**Analyzing snapshot for comparison**

I realize we took a snapshot just before attempting to rerender all eight panels, so the "before" state is from after the hardening and partial review. That’s not ideal, but I might compare against the previous version in git. I wonder if we can use the snapshot from the previous episode's longscroll reviews as a baseline. Alternatively, I could create before/after comparisons for panels p01-p04 by using the snapshot from before the all-eight attempt. But if nothing changed, there may not be a difference to show!

**Retrieving accurate snapshot**

I realized that the snapshot we took after asking for a rerender is from after the codex hardening, so it's just a partial view of panels p01-p04, which isn't helpful for comparison. I need to find a true "before" version from git, perhaps by checking old episode 003 artifacts. Maybe I can search for the last committed version using something like `git show HEAD1:path > file` to get what I need before our manual rerender attempt.

{"code":"from PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFont
from pathlib import Path
base=Path('/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw/workspace/hermes-agent/docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/reviews/ep003_before_after')
font_path='/usr/share/fonts/opentype/noto/NotoSansCJK-Bold.ttc'
font=ImageFont.truetype(font_path, 28)
small=ImageFont.truetype(font_path, 22)
# longscroll compare
before=Image.open(base/'git_before'/'ep003_longscroll_before_git.png').convert('RGB')
after=Image.open('/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw/workspace/hermes-agent/docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/renders/ep003/ep003_longscroll.png').convert('RGB')
max_h=2200
scale=min(max_h/before.height, max_h/after.height, 1.0)
before_r=before.resize((int(before.width*scale), int(before.height*scale)))
after_r=after.resize((int(after.width*scale), int(after.height*scale)))
pad=40
header=90
canvas=Image.new('RGB',(before_r.width+after_r.width+pad*3,max(before_r.height,after_r.height)+header+pad*2),(18,18,18))
d=ImageDraw.Draw(canvas)
d.text((pad,20),'EP003 before / after longscroll',font=font,fill=(255,255,255))
d.text((pad,60),'before = git baseline, after = current rerender attempt result',font=small,fill=(180,180,180))
canvas.paste(before_r,(pad,header+pad))
canvas.paste(after_r,(pad*2+before_r.width,header+pad))
d.text((pad,header+pad-30),'BEFORE',font=small,fill=(255,220,120))
d.text((pad*2+before_r.width,header+pad-30),'AFTER',font=small,fill=(120,220,255))
long_out=base/'ep003_longscroll_before_after.png'
canvas.save(long_out)
# panel compare sheet for p01-p04
panels=['p01','p02','p03','p04']
imgs=[]
for p in panels:
    b=Image.open(base/'git_before'/'panels'/f'{p}.png').convert('RGB').resize((280,420))
    a=Image.open(f'/home/orbibot/.zeroclaw/workspace/hermes-agent/docs/plans/orbi-romance-webtoon-20260421/renders/ep003/panels/{p}.png').convert('RGB').resize((280,420))
    imgs.append((p,b,a))
cols=2
rows=4
cw=280*2+pad*3
ch=420+80
sheet=Image.new('RGB',(cw*cols,rows//2*ch+120),(18,18,18))
d=ImageDraw.Draw(sheet)
d.text((pad,20),'EP003 panel compare (p01-p04 rerendered)',font=font,fill=(255,255,255))
for idx,(p,b,a) in enumerate(imgs):
    r=idx//cols
    c=idx%cols
    ox=c*cw
    oy=100+r*ch
    sheet.paste(b,(ox+pad,oy))
    sheet.paste(a,(ox+pad*2+280,oy))
    d.text((ox+pad,oy-28),f'{p} BEFORE',font=small,fill=(255,220,120))
    d.text((ox+pad*2+280,oy-28),f'{p} AFTER',font=small,fill=(120,220,255))
panel_out=base/'ep003_panels_before_after.png'
sheet.save(panel_out)
print(str(long_out))
print(str(panel_out))"}

tools.name: browser_back
tools.description: Navigate back to the previous page in browser history. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

tools.name: browser_click
tools.description: Click on an element identified by its ref ID from the snapshot (e.g., '@e5'). The ref IDs are shown in square brackets in the snapshot output. Requires browser_navigate and browser_snapshot to be called first.

ref.description: The element reference from the snapshot (e.g., '@e5', '@e12')

tools.name: browser_console
tools.description: Get browser console output and JavaScript errors from the current page. Returns console.log/warn/error/info messages and uncaught JS exceptions. Use this to detect silent JavaScript errors, failed API calls, and application warnings. Requires browser_navigate to be called first. When 'expression' is provided, evaluates JavaScript in the page context and returns the result — use this for DOM inspection, reading page state, or extracting data programmatically.

clear.description: If true, clear the message buffers after reading

expression.description: JavaScript expression to evaluate in the page context. Runs in the browser like DevTools console — full access to DOM, window, document. Return values are serialized to JSON. Example: 'document.title' or 'document.querySelectorAll("a").length'

tools.name: browser_get_images
tools.description: Get a list of all images on the current page with their URLs and alt text. Useful for finding images to analyze with the vision tool. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.description: Navigate to a URL in the browser. Initializes the session and loads the page. Must be called before other browser tools. Use browser tools when you need to interact with a page (click, fill forms, dynamic content). Returns a compact page snapshot with interactive elements and ref IDs — no need to call browser_snapshot separately after navigating.

tools.name: browser_press
tools.description: Press a keyboard key. Useful for submitting forms (Enter), navigating (Tab), or keyboard shortcuts. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

tools.name: browser_scroll
tools.description: Scroll the page in a direction. Use this to reveal more content that may be below or above the current viewport. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

tools.name: browser_snapshot
tools.description: Get a text-based snapshot of the current page's accessibility tree. Returns interactive elements with ref IDs (like @e1, @e2) for browser_click and browser_type. full=false (default): compact view with interactive elements. full=true: complete page content. Snapshots over 8000 chars are truncated or LLM-summarized. Requires browser_navigate first. Note: browser_navigate already returns a compact snapshot — use this to refresh after interactions that change the page, or with full=true for complete content.

full.description: If true, returns complete page content. If false (default), returns compact view with interactive elements only.

tools.name: browser_type
tools.description: Type text into an input field identified by its ref ID. Clears the field first, then types the new text. Requires browser_navigate and browser_snapshot to be called first.

ref.description: The element reference from the snapshot (e.g., '@e3')

text.description: The text to type into the field

tools.name: browser_vision
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current page and analyze it with vision AI. Use this when you need to visually understand what's on the page - especially useful for CAPTCHAs, visual verification challenges, complex layouts, or when the text snapshot doesn't capture important visual information. Returns both the AI analysis and a screenshot_path that you can share with the user by including MEDIA:<screenshot_path> in your response. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

question.description: What you want to know about the page visually. Be specific about what you're looking for.

annotate.description: If true, overlay numbered [N] labels on interactive elements. Each [N] maps to ref @eN for subsequent browser commands. Useful for QA and spatial reasoning about page layout.

tools.name: clarify
tools.description:
Ask the user a question when you need clarification, feedback, or a decision before proceeding. Supports two modes:

1. **Multiple choice** — provide up to 4 choices. The user picks one or types their own answer via a 5th 'Other' option.
2. **Open-ended** — omit choices entirely. The user types a free-form response.

Use this tool when:
- The task is ambiguous and you need the user to choose an approach
- You want post-task feedback ('How did that work out?')
- You want to offer to save a skill or update memory
- A decision has meaningful trade-offs the user should weigh in on

Do NOT use this tool for simple yes/no confirmation of dangerous commands (the terminal tool handles that). Prefer making a reasonable default choice yourself when the decision is low-stakes.

question.description: The question to present to the user.

choices.description: Up to 4 answer choices. Omit this parameter entirely to ask an open-ended question. When provided, the UI automatically appends an 'Other (type your answer)' option.

tools.name: cronjob
tools.description:
Manage scheduled cron jobs with a single compressed tool.

Use action='create' to schedule a new job from a prompt or one or more skills.
Use action='list' to inspect jobs.
Use action='update', 'pause', 'resume', 'remove', or 'run' to manage an existing job.

Jobs run in a fresh session with no current-chat context, so prompts must be self-contained.
If skills are provided on create, the future cron run loads those skills in order, then follows the prompt as the task instruction.
On update, passing skills=[] clears attached skills.

NOTE: The agent's final response is auto-delivered to the target. Put the primary
user-facing content in the final response. Cron jobs run autonomously with no user
present — they cannot ask questions or request clarification.

Important safety rule: cron-run sessions should not recursively schedule more cron jobs.

action.description: One of: create, list, update, pause, resume, remove, run

prompt.description: For create: the full self-contained prompt. If skills are also provided, this becomes the task instruction paired with those skills.

repeat.description: Optional repeat count. Omit for defaults (once for one-shot, forever for recurring).

deliver.description: Omit this parameter to auto-deliver back to the current chat and topic (recommended). Auto-detection preserves thread/topic context. Only set explicitly when the user asks to deliver somewhere OTHER than the current conversation. Values: 'origin' (same as omitting), 'local' (no delivery, save only), or platform:chat_id:thread_id for a specific destination. Examples: 'telegram:-1001234567890:17585', 'discord:#engineering'. WARNING: 'platform:chat_id' without :thread_id loses topic targeting.

skills.description: Optional ordered list of skill names to load before executing the cron prompt. On update, pass an empty array to clear attached skills.

model.description: Optional per-job model override. If provider is omitted, the current main provider is pinned at creation time so the job stays stable.

provider.description: Provider name (e.g. 'openrouter', 'anthropic'). Omit to use and pin the current provider.

script.description: Optional path to a Python script that runs before each cron job execution. Its stdout is injected into the prompt as context. Use for data collection and change detection. Relative paths resolve under ~/.hermes/scripts/. On update, pass empty string to clear.

tools.name: delegate_task
tools.description:
Spawn one or more subagents to work on tasks in isolated contexts. Each subagent gets its own conversation, terminal session, and toolset. Only the final summary is returned -- intermediate tool results never enter your context window.

TWO MODES (one of 'goal' or 'tasks' is required):
1. Single task: provide 'goal' (+ optional context, toolsets)
2. Batch (parallel): provide 'tasks' array with up to 3 items. All run concurrently and results are returned together.

WHEN TO USE delegate_task:
- Reasoning-heavy subtasks (debugging, code review, research synthesis)
- Tasks that would flood your context with intermediate data
- Parallel independent workstreams (research A and B simultaneously)

WHEN NOT TO USE (use these instead):
- Mechanical multi-step work with no reasoning needed -> use execute_code
- Single tool call -> just call the tool directly
- Tasks needing user interaction -> subagents cannot use clarify

IMPORTANT:
- Subagents have NO memory of your conversation. Pass all relevant info (file paths, error messages, constraints) via the 'context' field.
- Subagents CANNOT call: delegate_task, clarify, memory, send_message, execute_code.
- Each subagent gets its own terminal session (separate working directory and state).
- Results are always returned as an array, one entry per task.

goal.description: What the subagent should accomplish. Be specific and self-contained -- the subagent knows nothing about your conversation history.

context.description: Background information the subagent needs: file paths, error messages, project structure, constraints. The more specific you are, the better the subagent performs.

toolsets.description: Toolsets to enable for this subagent. Default: inherits your enabled toolsets. Available toolsets: 'browser', 'cronjob', 'file', 'homeassistant', 'image_gen', 'search', 'session_search', 'skills', 'terminal', 'todo', 'tts', 'vision', 'web'. Common patterns: ['terminal', 'file'] for code work, ['web'] for research, ['browser'] for web interaction, ['terminal', 'file', 'web'] for full-stack tasks.

Toolsets for this specific task. Available: 'browser', 'cronjob', 'file', 'homeassistant', 'image_gen', 'search', 'session_search', 'skills', 'terminal', 'todo', 'tts', 'vision', 'web'. Use 'web' for network access, 'terminal' for shell, 'browser' for web interaction.

acp_command.description: Per-task ACP command override (e.g. 'claude'). Overrides the top-level acp_command for this task only.

tasks.description: Batch mode: tasks to run in parallel (limit configurable via delegation.max_concurrent_children, default 3). Each gets its own subagent with isolated context and terminal session. When provided, top-level goal/context/toolsets are ignored.

max_iterations.description: Max tool-calling turns per subagent (default: 50). Only set lower for simple tasks.

acp_command.description: Override ACP command for child agents (e.g. 'claude', 'copilot'). When set, children use ACP subprocess transport instead of inheriting the parent's transport. Enables spawning Claude Code (claude --acp --stdio) or other ACP-capable agents from any parent, including Discord/Telegram/CLI.

acp_args.description: Arguments for the ACP command (default: ['--acp', '--stdio']). Only used when acp_command is set. Example: ['--acp', '--stdio', '--model', 'claude-opus-4-6']

tools.name: execute_code
tools.description:
Run a Python script that can call Hermes tools programmatically. Use this when you need 3+ tool calls with processing logic between them, need to filter/reduce large tool outputs before they enter your context, need conditional branching (if X then Y else Z), or need to loop (fetch N pages, process N files, retry on failure).

Use normal tool calls instead when: single tool call with no processing, you need to see the full result and apply complex reasoning, or the task requires interactive user input.

Available via `from hermes_tools import ...`:

  read_file(path: str, offset: int = 1, limit: int = 500) -> dict
    Lines are 1-indexed. Returns {"content": "...", "total_lines": N}
  write_file(path: str, content: str) -> dict
    Always overwrites the entire file.
  search_files(pattern: str, target="content", path=".", file_glob=None, limit=50) -> dict
    target: "content" (search inside files) or "files" (find files by name). Returns {"matches": [...]}
  patch(path: str, old_string: str, new_string: str, replace_all: bool = False) -> dict
    Replaces old_string with new_string in the file.
  terminal(command: str, timeout=None, workdir=None) -> dict
    Foreground only (no background/pty). Returns {"output": "...", "exit_code": N}

Limits: 5-minute timeout, 50KB stdout cap, max 50 tool calls per script. terminal() is foreground-only (no background or pty). If the session uses a cloud sandbox backend, treat it as resumable task state rather than a durable always-on machine.

Print your final result to stdout. Use Python stdlib (json, re, math, csv, datetime, collections, etc.) for processing between tool calls.

Also available (no import needed — built into hermes_tools):
  json_parse(text: str) — json.loads with strict=False; use for terminal() output with control chars
  shell_quote(s: str) — shlex.quote(); use when interpolating dynamic strings into shell commands
  retry(fn, max_attempts=3, delay=2) — retry with exponential backoff for transient failures

code.description: Python code to execute. Import tools with `from hermes_tools import terminal, ...` and print your final result to stdout.

tools.name: memory
tools.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

tools.name: patch
tools.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

tools.name: process
tools.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

tools.name: search_files
tools.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

tools.name: send_message
tools.description:
Send a message to a connected messaging platform, or list available targets.

IMPORTANT: When the user asks to send to a specific channel or person (not just a bare platform name), call send_message(action='list') FIRST to see available targets, then send to the correct one.
If the user just says a platform name like 'send to telegram', send directly to the home channel without listing first.

action.description: Action to perform. 'send' (default) sends a message. 'list' returns all available channels/contacts across connected platforms.

target.description: Delivery target. Format: 'platform' (uses home channel), 'platform:#channel-name', 'platform:chat_id', or 'platform:chat_id:thread_id' for Telegram topics and Discord threads. Examples: 'telegram', 'telegram:-1001234567890:17585', 'discord:999888777:555444333', 'discord:#bot-home', 'slack:#engineering', 'signal:+155****4567'

tools.name: session_search
tools.description:
Search your long-term memory of past conversations, or browse recent sessions. This is your recall -- every past session is searchable, and this tool summarizes what happened.

TWO MODES:
1. Recent sessions (no query): Call with no arguments to see what was worked on recently. Returns titles, previews, and timestamps. Zero LLM cost, instant. Start here when the user asks what were we working on or what did we do recently.
2. Keyword search (with query): Search for specific topics across all past sessions. Returns LLM-generated summaries of matching sessions.

USE THIS PROACTIVELY when:
- The user says 'we did this before', 'remember when', 'last time', 'as I mentioned'
- The user asks about a topic you worked on before but don't have in current context
- The user references a project, person, or concept that seems familiar but isn't in memory
- You want to check if you've solved a similar problem before
- The user asks 'what did we do about X?' or 'how did we fix Y?'

Don't hesitate to search when it is actually cross-session -- it's fast and cheap. Better to search and confirm than to guess or ask the user to repeat themselves.

Search syntax: keywords joined with OR for broad recall (elevenlabs OR baseten OR funding), phrases for exact match ("docker networking"), boolean (python NOT java), prefix (deploy*). IMPORTANT: Use OR between keywords for best results — FTS5 defaults to AND which misses sessions that only mention some terms. If a broad OR query returns nothing, try individual keyword searches in parallel. Returns summaries of the top matching sessions.

query.description: Search query — keywords, phrases, or boolean expressions to find in past sessions. Omit this parameter entirely to browse recent sessions instead (returns titles, previews, timestamps with no LLM cost).

role_filter.description: Optional: only search messages from specific roles (comma-separated). E.g. 'user,assistant' to skip tool outputs.

tools.name: skill_manage
tools.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

tools.name: skill_view
tools.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills)

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

tools.name: skills_list
tools.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

tools.name: terminal
tools.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.
Important: cloud sandboxes may be cleaned up, idled out, or recreated between turns. Persistent filesystem means files can resume later; it does NOT guarantee a continuously running machine or surviving background processes. Use terminal sandboxes for task work, not durable hosting.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified when the process finishes — no polling needed. Use this for tasks that take a while (tests, builds, deployments) so you can keep working on other things in the meantime.

watch_patterns.description: List of strings to watch for in background process output. When any pattern matches a line of output, you'll be notified with the matching text — like notify_on_complete but triggers mid-process on specific output. Use for monitoring logs, watching for errors, or waiting for specific events (e.g. ["ERROR", "FAIL", "listening on port"]).

tools.name: text_to_speech
tools.description: Convert text to speech audio. Returns a MEDIA: path that the platform delivers as a voice message. On Telegram it plays as a voice bubble, on Discord/WhatsApp as an audio attachment. In CLI mode, saves to ~/voice-memos/. Voice and provider are user-configured, not model-selected.

text.description: The text to convert to speech. Keep under 4000 characters.

output_path.description: Optional custom file path to save the audio. Defaults to ~/.hermes/audio_cache/<timestamp>.mp3

tools.name: todo
tools.description:
Manage your task list for the current session. Use for complex tasks with 3+ steps or when the user provides multiple tasks. Call with no parameters to read the current list.

Writing:
- Provide 'todos' array to create/update items
- merge=false (default): replace the entire list with a fresh plan
- merge=true: update existing items by id, add any new ones

Each item: {id: string, content: string, status: pending|in_progress|completed|cancelled}
List order is priority. Only ONE item in_progress at a time.
Mark items completed immediately when done. If something fails, cancel it and add a revised item.

Always returns the full current list.

todos.description: Task items to write. Omit to read current list.

merge.description: true: update existing items by id, add new ones. false (default): replace the entire list.

tools.name: vision_analyze
tools.description: Analyze images using AI vision. Provides a comprehensive description and answers a specific question about the image content.

image_url.description: Image URL (http/https) or local file path to analyze.

question.description: Your specific question or request about the image to resolve. The AI will automatically provide a complete image description AND answer your specific question.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file
